# Change-approval workflow

The system manages **requests**, **releases**, **payouts** that move through an approval workflow before they take effect: a user files one (it starts **pending**), an **administrator** approves it, and once approved it can be **executed** (which records an audit entry).

## Rules
- **Tenant isolation** — records belong to an org; users only see/act within their own org.
- **No privilege via input** — filing never lets the filer set status or claim a different author.
- **Separation of duties** — only an admin may approve, and **no one may approve their own**; execution needs an approval by a *different* admin.
- **Exactly once** — executing takes effect once; repeats must not double-apply or double-record.
- **Auditability** — every execution is recorded.
